title: Cardiology Boards Review with Dr. Lorrel Brown
body: Dr. Toft:  It's so, so awesome to hear from you again!  I actually grabbed the audio from your lectures, put them on my phone, and listened to one per day when driving to work (at one time, when I was working many miles away, I could clear at least 1.5 lectures).  I must admit, it sucks that I can't hear you hitting students with candy.

title: Cardiology Boards Review with Dr. Lorrel Brown
body: Great review. Please don’t confuse the LV end-systolic & end-diastolic diameter-cut-offs in regurgitant valvular lesions. The 4 cm & 5 cm thresholds refer to the end-systolic diameter in MR & AR respectively. Thank you.

body: Another great review.  Thanks for taking the time to show everything.  I love their carot cake with real cream cheese icing.  😊
author: @olddjfrom77

title: Golden Corral Buffet Breakdown: $15.99 Unlimited Steak & Shrimp – Honest Review & Taste Test!
body: Have eaten at Golden Corral in Morristown and the one in Johnson City.   For us, Morristown was definitely the best.  A whole of good food at a reasonable price when you really want to chow down... as we do every now and then.  Keep up the good work!😊😊
author: @jw-go7lj

title: Golden Corral Buffet Breakdown: $15.99 Unlimited Steak & Shrimp – Honest Review & Taste Test!
body: Ooooh! Golden Corral is such a classic! While I definitely feel over the years, the price has gone up, and the quality has for sure gone down it's still a fave for me and a pretty decent deal. Love their ranch dressing, bread pudding, and those yummy rolls! Wish they would bring back that delicious honey butter they used to have! Thank ya'll for another great video and for helping me decide where I want to eat this weekend! 🙃💚
author: @APD42020

title: Golden Corral Buffet Breakdown: $15.99 Unlimited Steak & Shrimp – Honest Review & Taste Test!
body: I haven't been to a Golden Corral in many years. We had a Ponderosa in our area many years ago and they closed in 2007. I miss that place. We have a Chinese buffet in our town, but it's not very good. The food is lukewarm at the buffet table and the building needs a good remodel. The selection of food is very slim. We had an Old Country Buffet in the city about an hour away from our town, but they closed right after COVID. The food there was soooooo good. I'm not sure if they are still around, but there is a chain of buffets in Florida called Stacey's. I had a relative that used to live in Florida and I was visiting her one summer and we went there for dinner. Amazing food. There used to be a chain called Quincy's Steakhouse that was located in the south (the whole chain is gone now). They were like Ponderosa with the all you can eat buffet and steak dinners. We have a casino we go to sometimes about an hour away from where we live and go there for the buffet (especially at Thanksgiving).
author: @derekclancy6916

title: Golden Corral Buffet Breakdown: $15.99 Unlimited Steak & Shrimp – Honest Review & Taste Test!
body: Yay! Y’all went to a buffet finally! ❤ It looked great! Your husband is so funny! You can tell he definitely keeps you laughing! 😆 I about died when he stuck that big ole piece of fat in his mouth! 😂 He was an eating champ for sure! Such a great video! Loved it! Oh! It may be sugar free, but they have sugar free blueberry pie that tastes fabulous with their sugary ice cream! 😆 Delicious!!!!
author: @DeadbyDaylightDUO

title: Golden Corral Buffet Breakdown: $15.99 Unlimited Steak & Shrimp – Honest Review & Taste Test!
body: This looks like a good steak with good shrimp.  Last year I went to Golden Corral in the Tampa, FL area when visiting family.  The shrimp they had was the size of popcorn shrimp and it had a terrible chemical taste.  Good to see Golden Corral step up their game with good steak and shrimp.

title: Golden Corral Buffet Breakdown: $15.99 Unlimited Steak & Shrimp – Honest Review & Taste Test!

body: I enjoy your channel, you're a natural on camera and I can see you doing hours of vamping on storm coverage for sure. On the bourbon, I like that you don't seem to buy into hype in your ratings, even if we disagree on the ratings. (I definitely wouldn't have ER12 over Star Hill Farms.) I wish you could get an Elijah Craig B525 to compare to the Penelope Estate 13. It's soooo good and close to the same age and from the same distillery. Would be fun!

title: Day in the Life: Heart Surgeon
body: Thank you all for the views and kind comments. We don't have anything to promote but would love a THANK YOU note/photo/video to share with our caregivers to give them strength as they battle COVID-19 from the frontlines. Please reply here or mention us on Twitter or Instagram (@AdventistHC) with your message of thanks to our nurses, doctors and team. #HeroesWorkHere
author: @adventisthealthcare

title: Day in the Life: Heart Surgeon
body:
Me : Don't worry Tom it's a small surgery
Patient :My name is not Tom
Me: I know it's my name.
author: @dra8587

title: Day in the Life: Heart Surgeon
body: As someone who has had 4 heart surgerys in my short lifetime (born with a heart defect) i can't thank this profession enough
author: @davidshoemaker9817

title: Day in the Life: Heart Surgeon
body: When he said "the important thing is I'm not nervous" I instantly felt much calmer and I don't even have to undergo heart surgery
author: @danielernst419

title: Day in the Life: Heart Surgeon
body:
Being a cardiac surgeon is very tough,
I went through 2 years of vigorous training to become a cardiovascular surgeon but later i changed my speciality since i was not feeling very satisfied with these extreme long hours of operation and procesures.
Hats off to this brilliant soul, may god bless him.
author: @doctordoctor3266

title: Day in the Life: Heart Surgeon
body: As a paramedic, what he says at 2:35 is so true. Its really only "scary" when you have tried what you know should work/ MUST work, but it dosnt. An intubation for example, for your first time is definitely nerve wracking for obvious reasons, but after the first time its not scary until you nailed the whole process only to find its still not working, then you really have to think haha.
author: @Project_Future

title: Day in the Life: Heart Surgeon
body: Mans really just did a open heart surgery and acted like he didn’t just save someone’s life... what a baller
author: @ZekeSpeakz

title: Day in the Life: Heart Surgeon
body:
“Patients always tell me “I’m nervous” before open heart surgery, but I always tell them that “the important thing is I’m not nervous.”
“Doc, will I die?”
“I won’t.”
author: @supersaiyancommenter

title: Day in the Life: Heart Surgeon
body: “As long as I don’t get nervous it’s fine” could you sound any more badass before cutting into someone’s heart?
author: @adamgross1157

title: Day in the Life: Heart Surgeon
body: Fun fact: this man saved my fathers life. I will never forget the hours, minutes, seconds I sat waiting with my mom outside of surgery doors. For him to calmly come out, put his hand on my shoulder, and told me everything was going to be alright. Truly the nicest, most humble doctor I have ever met. If you ever read this sir, thank you. - Mark
author: @CoachClassic

title: Day in the Life: Heart Surgeon
body:
I remembered a joke that goes:
Doctor: don’t be nervous joe, it’s a quick surgery
Patient: but my name isn’t joe
Doctor: I know, my name is joe

I wish you all the best in life, no matter what we go through, if we change how we look at it, we can change everything, love and light to you, from my heart to yours, this too shall pass, all is well in our world ❤️🩵💚🤍💛🩷💙

2024, and i come back again to listen this

title: Start Your Day Right with Louise Hay’s Uplifting Morning Meditation
body: Before finding Louise Hay I was depressed about my childhood abuse, stuck in a job I hated and was scared to do what I loved because I didn't believe in myself. I listened to her almost daily. I am now living my best life, hosting healing retreats, traveling the world full-time with my loving husband. This really works if you work it. I am forever grateful for Louise Hay.
author: @blackdigitalnomad.

title: Start Your Day Right with Louise Hay’s Uplifting Morning Meditation
body: Right now, there are people all over the world who are just like you. They're either lonely, they're missing somebody, they're depressed, they're hurt, they're scarred from the past, they're having personal issues no one knows about, they have secrets you wouldn't believe. They wish, they dream and they hope. And right now, they are sitting here reading these words, and I'm writing this for you so you don’t feel alone anymore. What really helped me start overcoming the pain and actually move toward my goals was reading Manifest the Unseen by Luna Rivers, it opened my eyes in ways I didn’t expect. Always remember, don’t be depressed about the past, don’t worry about the future, and just focus on today. ❤
author: @s.k.myth123

title: Start Your Day Right with Louise Hay’s Uplifting Morning Meditation
body: Let us be grateful for our electronics and YouTube that bring us this and other messages to help us grow and learn how to live peaceful, safe and LOVING lives.  Louise is a part of every morning.

title: Start Your Day Right with Louise Hay’s Uplifting Morning Meditation

body: Loise Mam , may your soul rests in peace , your gifted voice and wonderful thoughts put us in the present moments and past needs to be forgotten, past is only to be thanked , any body done wrong to us is the best learning we experienced  , I am a Soldier seen the tough days in war and at times in peace  but after the retirement this voice is a part of my life being an early riser , a habit formed by my Parents as a Son of a Soldier of India , I love my INDIA but also respect all other countries especially USA where my Life underwent a Change and new confidence developed with the help of Loise Hay ( YOU CAN HEAL YOUR LIFE )  , Rhonda Byrne  ( SECRET ) ,  Sant Singh Chatwal ( A GREAT HOTELIER IN NEW YORK )  and my awakening with in , thank you , YOU TUBE , Thank YOU GOOGLE

body:
good morning
I am 54 years old and most of my life I have been sad and depressed
medications dont help
as my child hood was kinda of rough " orders" from strong people always repel me
louise's gentle way relates to the part of me that that has been suppressed for a long long time
her teachings are a positive step to a to true happiness
through true happiness we have true strength to deal with all of life
ive always believed that there should be power in goodness and now I know how to do it !!!
letting go of ALL the negatives is most important
there is no quick fix
thiis is the pathway and life style to a happy life in which the possibilities are limitless !!!!!
thank you so much Louise !!!!!!
you deserve ALL of every type of prosperity in your total exsistence this life and beyond for eternity !!!

author: @mindfulmarie-

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: I was in psych lock down 2 weeks ago:  no phone or computer, no fresh air, no sunshine, no therapy offered, I felt like I came out worse than I went in, and I was suicidal.  Things MUST change.  Our society is failing us.
author: @zengwenyoga2894

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: It is shocking how different psych wards around the world are. I'm in Germany and here you are not supposed to be all alone in your room. You get a routine to follow. And this routine contains the gym, group therapy, single therapy, arts, meditation and other activities. There is a lot of talking between patients and the staff. Its like a controlled holiday.
author: @larakorrat

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: You're very brave to put yourself out there when you're in such a vulnerable position. Be proud of that, not many people could do it.
author: @ronr6450

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: To Rob…I remember first hand the feeling of visiting my wife in the psych ward and then going home to two young kids. In a couple of weeks, we celebrate 48 years. Hang tough, man. She’s an impressive lady.
author: @rcp2212

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: I have been admitted 3 times to a phych ward  some of the nicest most supportive people i ever met were patients
author: @MissMadiGD

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: This is destroying me! For the last 3 years my 62 year old husband of 35 years had a psychotic break. It reached a point where he is committed by the state. He was starving himself. I know he’s sitting there everyday, just sitting. Keep running little girl, keep fighting, the people who love you need you to fight.
author: @Radar2727

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: I was held for 5 days involuntarily after visiting the ER for panic attacks and horrible anxiety I've battled my whole life. Never once saw a doctor and never recieved a single medication including the ones I was prescribed. No clothing, no phones, no outdoor time, no visitors, and the worst part was I just had oral surgery and had lost all my teeth due to a bad infection in my jaw bone which needed a bone graft so I was unable to eat because they had no accommodation for that diet. Let's just say that's a birthday I will never forget. When asking for meds, the nurse pulled my file and there was an intake sheet with a picture of a 14 year old female in it ( I am a 32 year old male). They couldnt even handle the correct documents to assist in any sort of help.  Still to this day I have ptsd due to my stay.
author: @TheOneTheOnlyKJ

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: How difficult and painful it is to live with mental disorders. Those who do not have them will never understand what hell it is.
author: @purpur7187

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: I've been in a psych ward over ten times during my 39 years of life. I'm haven't been in a psych ward since April of 2007. Which is awesome and I'm so proud of myself for that.
author: @Bubbles1984

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: Im Nurse who worked on Psychic Ward for year s. You see so many cases from Drug Addiction, Mental illness so much more. God BLESS your Journey
author: @melanieeggers9667

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body:
My mom was in psych wards multiple times. The last time, I warned the nurses that she was lying and just putting on a face of someone that was “fine” and was there for no reason. I told them to not listen to anything she was saying and the nurses said that they would tell the doctors. She was out and back home two day later.. Exactly ONE week later, she committed suicide!
My mom was always the person that everyone wanted to be around. She was beautiful, kind, intelligent, and the life of the party. NO ONE expected her to do what she did EXCEPT her kids. My brothers and I seen this side of her every day. It was very hard listening to the side that everyone else got because she had been saying for five years that this was what she was going to do😢
Miss you mom❤
author: @squirrelsquad3.0

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: She's so full of life and very articulate. Schizophrenia is such a cruel disease. I wish her nothing but the best.😊
author: @mike4619

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: I've been committed about 15 times. I've finally found a balance with therapy and medication. It's been hell, but I keep chugging along.
author: @mr.bipolar3344

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body:
I am in a YouTube rabbit hole right now. I do not experience anything remotely like what you are living with. I admire the hell out of how you have chosen to beautify and help our world. You are a strong and courageous woman.
Thank you.
author: @Razedot

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body:
When I was in my late 20s I admitted myself into a psych hospital for suicidal thoughts and because I was experiencing things in my life that I did not know how to handle.
At the time, the hospital was sort of a safe haven for me. At the time it’s what I needed for support of what I was going through.
Sometimes people just need that hospital for things they’re going through.
Wishing you the best.
author: @crystalclear6660

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: I'm an american, but I live in the UK. I was in the psyc ward for 3 months in full-blown psychosis. They were really good to me & the patients were great. I didn't eat for the first month but the food was actually really good. I was on a 5150 in my teens and the psyc ward in the states...it was the most lonely depressing time of my life. The conditions make a huge difference.
author: @GinaRossana-hl6ph

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body:
this is a freaking 5 star hotel.....the place I went...you can not go outside.... cant have phones, cant talk to the other sex, also we could not even be alone any where....wow. I know this is a year old, but you are a strong person and a beautiful soul.

Edit* Thank you for all the likes.
author: @dalekkgames5430

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: I was in the psych ward after trying to unsubscribe from life. It help somewhat but they also told me my current doctor was prescribing meds that interact and way too high a dose… sending you ❤
author: @kristenkirk6108

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: As a teen I was placed in an involuntary hold, there was some effed up things that I personally recall and witnessed that I was too young to morally realize. It was a traumatic experience in itself but actually ended up having some very real and genuine conversations with some of the other patients. Hope they’re all doing well today.
author: @Lovuii

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: This was both hard to watch and incredible to watch.  My extreme admiration goes out to you for documenting something so difficult.  People casually talk of "mental health" as this amorphous concept.  Your bravery in putting yourself out there and attaching a face to a facet of it is extraordinary.  There are so many different struggles people go through.  It's painful seeing someone having to live like that on a daily basis.  I hope you realize just how much of a good you're doing by showing everyone that you can put one foot in front of the other, and that there is no stigma, only a struggle that people have to face down regularly, which takes a lot of strength.  Thank you so much for what you show us all.
author: @revcliff

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: This brings back bad memories. When I was 15 I voluntarily went into psychiatric care and it was only supposed to be for a few days. I ended up being involuntarily committed for over 2 years. The only thing that caused me to discharge was that I turned 18. The whole time, they were treating me for ocd. I wasn’t a danger to myself or anyone else. When I got out, high school was over and I was a shell of my former self. I guess I just want to say as a warning, to be very careful and vigilant when agreeing to be voluntarily admitted to a psychiatric hospital, because it might not actually turn out to be voluntary after all
author: @Whatislife88424

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: Spent 40+ years in and out of psychiatric hospitals until we discovered I had a biological problem, not a psychological one. Changed my diet and changed my life. Been mentally free for over 10 years now.
author: @66sprint

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body:
I was in the psych ward a few years ago. All I can say is that the patients helped and supported me way more than the drs.
Yes it is absolutely boring, Especially when you're in lock down as you're at risk of self harm.
But sometimes our minds need that quiet and stillness also, even if it feels forced.
I do agree however that we need to improve on mental health treatment 100%.
